description_long: This object is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic taildragger configuration. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the midsection of the fuselage, with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The nose features a prominent multi-blade propeller, and the cockpit is positioned just behind the propeller, enclosed with a smooth canopy. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The landing gear is not extended, but the structure suggests a taildragger arrangement typical of vintage or WWII-era fighter aircraft.
description: A streamlined, single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a classic taildragger tail and enclosed cockpit. The design is reminiscent of vintage fighter aircraft, with a smooth fuselage and conventional tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, straight wings, central propeller, and streamlined nose.
description_view_1: Left-side perspective highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit canopy, and taildragger tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, including the single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_3: Right-side perspective showing the propeller, wing, and the smooth transition from fuselage to tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings, no visible winglets, and minimal dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a multi-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration, main gear forward and a single tail wheel, gear retracted or not visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, positioned just behind the propeller.
